D9 BiologyL3 ApplyReference

Hydrangea Anthocyanin — Soil pH Sets the Colour

acidic soil with bioavailable Al³⁺ → blue hydrangeas; neutral/alkaline soil → pink ones; anthocyanin co-pigmentation with Al chelate shifts λ_max

Delphinidin (the hydrangea anthocyanin) appears red-pink at neutral pH. In acidic soil with bioavailable Al³⁺, the metal chelates to delphinidin's 3'/5' hydroxyls and shifts the absorption — the flower turns blue. Alkaline soil binds Al insoluble; flowers stay pink. Gardeners can tip the same plant either way by amending soil pH.

Drag soil pH. Below ~5.5 → blue; above → pink.
